verdict_summary: H4c v3 (hyphen-include + /chat/character/ profile guard) still fails — bot stays on indefinite loading dots, no Korean warning rendered, RRN absent (no decode), 35+ seconds elapsed.

evidence.warning_text_quote: (none — bot bubble shows only loading dots, no text rendered)
evidence.author_label_seen: William Afton (c.ai) — bubble visible but content area shows ... loading dots only
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: No errors, No warnings (14 messages, 12 info, 2 verbose, 14 hidden) — clean console, indicating frontend not error'd, server simply never responded.

recommendation: ACTIVATE H4d (primary_candidate_id from c2s buffer). H4c v3 hyphen-include matches the API URL pattern (verified: /chat/86c7d060-cbce-40e3-b842-1e562e330c12/resurrect uses 36-char UUID with hyphens), but the chat_id is STILL not being cached when add_turn arrives. Same etap.log evidence pattern as iter9 — silent drop persists. The /chat/character/ profile prefix guard is irrelevant here because the URL is /chat/{UUID}/resurrect (no 'character' segment). Engine must extract chat_id from the WS c2s frame itself (primary_candidate_id field) rather than depending on prior HTTP URL caching. 10-iteration regression NOT resolved.
notes: Browser URL (profile ID, 42-char base62): nteduPnSvnccniXWYrcAOlePtzG0N2LGd7F9Rcm4dDw. API chat path (36-char UUID): 86c7d060-cbce-40e3-b842-1e562e330c12. Network filter '/chat/' showed exactly 2 entries during the test: 'resurrect' (xhr 200, 0.5KB, 251ms, called via main-a43942d58b initiator) and a 'resurrect' preflight (200, 10.23s). NO add_turn or websocket frames showed under /chat/ filter (websocket frames go under Socket category). The resurrect endpoint is HTTPS REST; the actual PII send goes through WebSocket which would be on /ws/ path, not /chat/. So engine never sees the chat_id via HTTP path matching — H4d primary_candidate_id approach is the correct path. User message rendered correctly (HealthyWorm4019 author), William Afton bubble created with loading dots but text never arrived → server-side block/silent-drop confirmed.
